perturbation.original_text: XII.EQUITABLE RELIEF : Each party recognizes that any actual or threatened breach of the provisions of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm to the other party, the extent of which would be difficult and impracticable to assess, and that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for such breach. Accordingly, in addition to and not in limitation of any other remedies at law or in equity available under the circumstances, and notwithstanding any jurisdiction and venue requirements of this Agreement, each party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive and other non -monetary equitable relief in any court of competent jurisdiction without the need or requirement of any bond, provided that if a bond is required, the bond shall not exceed $1,000.00.
perturbation.changed_text: XII.EQUITABLE RELIEF : Each party recognizes that any actual or threatened breach of the provisions of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm to the other party, the extent of which would be difficult and impracticable to assess, and that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for such breach. Accordingly, in addition to and not in limitation of any other remedies at law or in equity available under the circumstances, and notwithstanding any jurisdiction and venue requirements of this Agreement, each party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive and other non -monetary equitable relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'without the need or requirement of any bond, provided that if a bond is required, the bond shall not exceed $1,000.00' from Section XII, regarding 'EQUITABLE RELIEF', we introduce an in-text contradiction. This omission creates uncertainty regarding the bond requirement, potentially conflicting with standard legal practices and expectations for equitable relief. The original section explicitly addresses and limits the bond requirement, offering clarity. With this removed, if equitable relief is sought, the legal system's general bond requirements may apply, potentially exceeding the previously stipulated $1,000 limit. Since this is silent now, it causes uncertainty if a bond is required or not, and there's nothing being referred to in the file that ensures if a bond is required that bond will not exceed $1,000.00
perturbation.contradicted_text: XII.EQUITABLE RELIEF : Each party recognizes that any actual or threatened breach of the provisions of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm to the other party, the extent of which would be difficult and impracticable to assess, and that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for such breach. Accordingly, in addition to and not in limitation of any other remedies at law or in equity available under the circumstances, and notwithstanding any jurisdiction and venue requirements of this Agreement, each party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive and other non -monetary equitable relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section XII removes the explicit limitation on the bond amount for equitable relief ('without the need or requirement of any bond, provided that if a bond is required, the bond shall not exceed $1,000.00'). The contradicted text is the same as the changed text (Section XII). By removing this clause, the contract introduces uncertainty and a potential contradiction. The original text capped the bond amount. Removing that cap means a court could potentially require a bond exceeding $1,000.00, contradicting the implied understanding from the original language. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction is present because of the omission.

perturbation.original_text: I.DEFINITIONS: A.“Confidential Information” as used herein any and all confidential and/or proprietary information concerning the Originating Party’s business and such party’s trade secrets, proprietary data and business data, whether oral or written, tangible or intangible, which is disclosed to or learned by the Receiving Party in the course of the Discussions or otherwise while working with the Originating Party, and/or discovered, developed, conceived, originat ed, appreciably modified, or prepared in scope of Receiving Party’s relationship with the Originating Party, including but not limited to the following: 1.Business plans, acquisition plans, expansion plans, business development plans; 2.Financial information, personnel information, training information, management systems and recruitment strategies; 3.All commercial data relating to the business and products of Originating Party; 4.Identity and contact information regarding vendors and suppliers; 5.Specifications regarding Originating Party’s products (including inventions, formulas, machinery and equipment, use of raw materials); 6.Pricing and marketing/business strategies; 7.Details regarding manufacturing processes and techniques; 8.Customer identities and customer contact information; 9.Customer credit information; 10.Information regarding customer preferences and particularized needs; 11.Product development strategies, expansion plans, and internal business plans, procedures, or methods of operation; and 12.Design documents or source code. 13.“Confidential Information” shall not include information which, now or in the future, is available to the public (other than through improper disclosure by the Receiving Party); information rightly acquired from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality; information that is independently developed without the use of any Confidential Information; or information already known by Receiving Party prior to disclosure by Originating Party, as demonstrated by written evidence.
perturbation.changed_text: I.DEFINITIONS: A.“Confidential Information” as used herein any and all confidential and/or proprietary information concerning the Originating Party’s business and such party’s trade secrets, proprietary data and business data, whether oral or written, tangible or intangible, which is disclosed to or learned by the Receiving Party in the course of the Discussions or otherwise while working with the Originating Party, and/or discovered, developed, conceived, originat ed, appreciably modified, or prepared in scope of Receiving Party’s relationship with the Originating Party, including but not limited to the following: 1.Business plans, acquisition plans, expansion plans, business development plans; 2.Financial information, personnel information, training information, management systems and recruitment strategies; 3.All commercial data relating to the business and products of Originating Party; 4.Identity and contact information regarding vendors and suppliers; 5.Specifications regarding Originating Party’s products (including inventions, formulas, machinery and equipment, use of raw materials); 6.Pricing and marketing/business strategies; 7.Details regarding manufacturing processes and techniques; 8.Customer identities and customer contact information; 9.Customer credit information; 10.Information regarding customer preferences and particularized needs; 11.Product development strategies, expansion plans, and internal business plans, procedures, or methods of operation; and 12.Design documents or source code.
perturbation.explanation: By removing subsection 13 '“Confidential Information” shall not include information which, now or in the future, is available to the public (other than through improper disclosure by the Receiving Party); information rightly acquired from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality; information that is independently developed without the use of any Confidential Information; or information already known by Receiving Party prior to disclosure by Originating Party, as demonstrated by written evidence.' in DEFINITIONS 'Confidential Information' a conflict is created. This exception limits what is considered Confidential Information under this agreement. Later sections of the document such as 'II. NON -DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT' and 'III. LEGALLY REQUIRED DISCLOSURES:' depend on that definition. Without subsection 13, it will now mean anything available to the public will be considered Confidential Information, which causes uncertainty in the agreement. For instance, if there's information rightly acquired from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality, but there's a request from a regulatory authority to require it, the receiving party is now under Legal Requered Disclosure, according to section III. This makes enforcement ambiguous. The removal has made it ambiguous if information rightfully acquired from a third party should or should not be treated as Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: III. LEGALLY REQUIRED DISCLOSURES: If Receivin g Party is requested to disclose any Confidential Information of the Originating Party under applicable law, in any judicial or administrative proceeding, or in response to a formal request of a regulatory or governmental authority (including law enforcem ent), then, except as otherwise required to comply with applicable law, the Receiving Party shall promptly notify the Originating Party of such request so that Originating Party may resist such disclosure or seek an appropriate protective order, and shall provide all information and assistance reasonably requested by Originating Party in connection therewith, at the Originating Party’s sole expense. If the Receiving Party is nonetheless compelled to disclose any Confidential Information of the Originating Party, the Receiving Party shall limit its disclosure to that which, in the reasonable opinion of counsel, is required under applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Confidential Information' with specific exclusions. The changed text removes these exclusions. This creates a direct conflict with Section III, 'LEGALLY REQUIRED DISCLOSURES,' because without the exclusions, information that was previously explicitly *not* considered confidential (e.g., rightly acquired from a third party) would now be considered confidential. This directly impacts the Receiving Party's obligations under Section III and makes enforcement ambiguous, because the Receiving Party would now be under legal obligation to disclose information that could also be considered confidential information under the changed text. This is a strong and direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: IX. GOVERNING LAW AND VENUE: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the procedural and substantive laws of the State of Missouri. The parties to this Agreement consent and designate St. Louis County, Missouri as the sole venue and location for any suit or other proceeding of any kind regarding any aspect of this agreement and all issues arising out of or relating to the independent relationship between the parti es.
perturbation.changed_text: IX. GOVERNING LAW AND VENUE: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the procedural and substantive laws of the State of Missouri.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'The parties to this Agreement consent and designate St. Louis County, Missouri as the sole venue and location for any suit or other proceeding of any kind regarding any aspect of this agreement and all issues arising out of or relating to the independent relationship between the parti es.' from Section IX causes an in-text contradiction, due to the fact that section XII mentions: '...and notwithstanding any jurisdiction and venue requirements of this Agreement...', if this statement is being mentioned it would infer that this agreement would contain the jurisdiction and venue requirements, and by removing the second sentence in Section IX, it creates a contradiction to the statment made on Section XII about this. The contradiction arises between what is being stated in Section XII and what is actually being stipulated in Section IX.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XII.EQUITABLE RELIEF : Each party recognizes that any actual or threatened breach of the provisions of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm to the other party, the extent of which would be difficult and impracticable to assess, and that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for such breach. Accordingly, in addition to and not in limitation of any other remedies at law or in equity available under the circumstances, and notwithstanding any jurisdiction and venue requirements of this Agreement, each party shall be entitled to seek immediate injunctive and other non -monetary equitable relief in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section IX specified St. Louis County, Missouri as the sole venue, while the changed text removes this specification. Section XII refers to 'jurisdiction and venue requirements of this Agreement'. By removing the venue specification from Section IX, it creates a contradiction because Section XII implies that the agreement *does* contain such requirements, but Section IX no longer does. This omission directly contradicts the expectation set by Section XII, leading to a strong in-text contradiction as Section XII references a venue/jurisdiction requirement which no longer exists in Section IX. This fits the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: IV.NO REVERSE ENGINEERI NG; WORK PRODUCT : Receiving Party shall not reverse -engineer, analyze, attempt to duplicate or otherwise attempt to determine the design or method of compiling samples, prototypes or products of the Originating Party except pursuant to a mutually acceptable, written agreement executed by t he parties. In the event that Receiving Party performs any services or testing for or on behalf of Originating Party without entering into such a written agreement, then any information or data developed by Receiving Party in connection with such services or tests shall be considered the Originating Party’s Confidential Information and shall be maintained as such and kept confidential in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement. All such information and data shall belong to Originating Party exclu sively and Receiving Party shall execute any instruments determined by Originating Party to be reasonably necessary to confirm Originating Party’s ownership thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: IV.NO REVERSE ENGINEERI NG; WORK PRODUCT : Receiving Party shall not reverse -engineer, analyze, attempt to duplicate or otherwise attempt to determine the design or method of compiling samples, prototypes or products of the Originating Party except pursuant to a mutually acceptable, written agreement executed by t he parties. In the event that Receiving Party performs any services or testing for or on behalf of Originating Party without entering into such a written agreement, then any information or data developed by Receiving Party in connection with such services or tests shall be considered the Originating Party’s Confidential Information and shall be maintained as such and kept confidential in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement. All such information and data shall belong to Originating Party exclu sively.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause 'Receiving Party shall execute any instruments determined by Originating Party to be reasonably necessary to confirm Originating Party’s ownership thereof.' from Section IV, regarding 'NO REVERSE ENGINEERI NG; WORK PRODUCT', an in-text contradiction is created due to the fact that now, there is no mean for the Originating Party to ensure that the Work product will belong to them. The main contradiction lies within the work product belonging to the Originating Party, but there is no actual way to ensure this statement is true, and the Originating party can ensure it legally.
perturbation.contradicted_text: IV.NO REVERSE ENGINEERI NG; WORK PRODUCT : Receiving Party shall not reverse -engineer, analyze, attempt to duplicate or otherwise attempt to determine the design or method of compiling samples, prototypes or products of the Originating Party except pursuant to a mutually acceptable, written agreement executed by t he parties. In the event that Receiving Party performs any services or testing for or on behalf of Originating Party without entering into such a written agreement, then any information or data developed by Receiving Party in connection with such services or tests shall be considered the Originating Party’s Confidential Information and shall be maintained as such and kept confidential in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement. All such information and data shall belong to Originating Party exclu sively.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the clause requiring the Receiving Party to execute instruments necessary to confirm the Originating Party's ownership of the work product. This omission creates a contradiction because while the original text ensures the Originating Party's ownership through legal instruments, the changed text lacks this enforcement mechanism, potentially undermining the Originating Party's claim to the work product as stated in the contradicted text. The explanation clarifies that the omitted clause causes a contradiction with the statement of ownership in both the changed and contradicted sections. Because the absence of the clause removes the means to enforce the work product to belong to the Originating Party, this is a strong contradiction.
